text: US CENTCOM confirmed 50+ military targets struck on Kharg Island — bunkers, storage, air defense. Oil infra reportedly NOT targeted. IDF separately struck 10 bridges/railways across Iran. Kashan: 2 killed, 3 injured at Yahyaabad railway bridge.
verificationNote: NBC News, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post, ANI confirmed.

text: IDF struck Shiraz petrochemical complex — described as 'one of last remaining nitric acid production sites' critical for explosives and ballistic missiles. Also eliminated ballistic missile commander Makram Atimi in Kermanshah. Mobile launcher truck destroyed in Tabriz.
verificationNote: i24NEWS, YNet News, Jerusalem Post, Fox News confirmed.

text: TRUMP: 'A whole civilization will die tonight.' NETANYAHU: Passover 'Ten Plagues' address. PEZESHKIAN: 14M Iranians ready to die. Iran rejected ceasefire with 10 clauses. Hegseth impeachment push. Guterres warns civilian infra strikes unlawful. DEADLINE 8PM ET.
verificationNote: CNN, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, Axios, CGTN confirmed.

text: Explosions heard in Damascus — confirmed as Israeli air defense intercepting Iranian missiles over Syrian airspace. Petah Tikvah: 34-year-old woman seriously injured by interceptor debris. Southern Israel missiles intercepted. King Fahd Causeway reopened after overnight closure.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz, WTOP confirmed.

text: Day 39 PM summary: US 50+ targets Kharg Island. IDF 10 bridges/railways (Kashan 2 dead). Shiraz petrochemical hit. Atimi killed. Trump 'civilization dies tonight.' Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues.' Gas $4.14. Brent $111/WTI $115. OPEC+ 206K bpd. Hegseth impeachment. DEADLINE 8PM ET.
verificationNote: Compiled from NBC, Al Jazeera, CNN, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, AAA.

text: OPEC+ agreed +206K bpd increase for May. IEA 400M barrel emergency release — analysts call it 'Band-Aid' absorbed in 26 days. JPMorgan warns oil could hit $120-150 if Bab al-Mandeb closes. Urea fertilizer $590/MT (+19%). 25% of US farmers haven't bought fertilizer.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera, FAO confirmed.

text: BREAKING: Russia and China vetoed the UNSC Hormuz resolution. Vote 11-2 with 2 abstentions. Bahrain-sponsored resolution had already been watered down. Multilateral path to reopening the strait is now dead.
verificationNote: Confirmed by WaPo, PBS, Bloomberg, France24. Vote count verified.

text: IRGC launched 11 ballistic missiles and 18 drones at Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province in two waves. All missiles intercepted but debris struck SABIC petrochemical complex at Jubail. Fire visible on satellite imagery. Partial evacuations ordered.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al-Monitor, JPost, WION. Satellite imagery circulating.

text: Major intel leak: US/Israeli memo claims Mojtaba Khamenei is unconscious in Qom, unable to govern. Wounded in same Feb 28 strike that killed his father. Only AI-generated video shown. Raises critical question: who is actually running Iran's war effort?
verificationNote: Reported by JPost, Newsweek, GB News. Iranian officials deny. Intel memo existence confirmed but content unverified independently.

text: Iran officially severed all diplomatic and indirect communication channels with the US. Pakistan back-channel reportedly still active. Came hours after Trump's 'whole civilization will die tonight' threat and rejection of Iran's 10-point peace plan.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Iran International, WSJ, multiple outlets. Pakistan mediation channel reportedly continues.

text: King Fahd Causeway between Saudi Arabia and Bahrain temporarily closed due to Iranian air raid threats. US issued shelter-in-place for all Americans in Bahrain. Causeway is only road link to island nation hosting US Fifth Fleet.
verificationNote: Confirmed by WTOP, Jordan News. Causeway later reopened.

text: IDF struck Khorramshahr shipbuilding facilities — Arvan Shipbuilding and Shahid Mahdavi Marine Industries. These produce 80% of IRGC Navy surface combatants. Smoke plumes visible 20km away. Shatt al-Arab shipping halted.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Alma Center daily report and Kurdistan24.

text: Iran's 10-point peace plan calls for: permanent end to war (not temporary ceasefire), security guarantee, Hormuz reopening with ~$2M/ship toll, sanctions lifting, end to strikes on Hezbollah, reconstruction payment. Trump says 'not good enough.'
verificationNote: Full plan published by Al Jazeera and Gulf News. Trump response confirmed by CNN.

text: CEASEFIRE: Trump agrees to 2-week suspension of bombing Iran, conditional on 'COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz.' Pakistan PM Sharif mediated. Bloomberg confirms 'two-week ceasefire to finalize talks.' Major de-escalation after 39 days.
verificationNote: Confirmed by US News, CNBC, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, NBC News.

text: Iran launched 6 separate BM salvos at central/southern Israel on last day of Passover. ~10 missiles per salvo, most with cluster munitions. ~50 submunition impact sites across Petah Tikva, Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak. 6 wounded including 1 woman seriously injured.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog and FDD Long War Journal.

text: IDF joint IAF/Navy operation struck Iran's central sonar production facility in Shiraz + last remaining petrochemical plant producing nitric acid for BM warheads. IDF now claims 85%+ of Iran's petrochemical export capacity inoperable. Also struck large BM system site in NW Iran.
verificationNote: Confirmed by ANI News, The Media Line, IDF official statements.

text: Iranian drone strike hits Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait overnight — 15 American service members wounded. Most returned to duty. IRGC claims as Wave 98 of Operation True Promise 4. US total wounded now 380 across 7 countries.
verificationNote: Confirmed by APA, Al Mayadeen, GlobalSecurity.

text: Day 39 comprehensive: IDF struck 10+ railway segments/bridges, 3 Tehran airports, Shiraz sonar + petrochemical, Tabriz, Khorramabad, Isfahan, Kashan. Iranian UAV hit Ali al-Salem (15 wounded). 7 BMs at Saudi Arabia intercepted. 12 Iranian attack waves at Israel Apr 6-7.
verificationNote: Alma Center daily report April 7.

text: Day 39 late PM: Trump 2-week ceasefire bombshell via Pakistan. Pope Leo calls civilization threat 'truly unacceptable.' Vance: Iran faces 'two pathways.' Hegseth claims contradict reality per officials. Brent $113.40, WTI $115.17. Gas $4.14. Hormuz Day 36.
verificationNote: Compiled from US News, CNBC, Vatican News, Washington Times, Spokesman-Review.

text: CEASEFIRE CONFIRMED: Iran's SNSC formally accepted the two-week ceasefire. FM Araghchi says Hormuz safe passage 'via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.' China gave 'last-minute nudge.' This is NOT a full reopening — Iran retains control authority over all transit.
verificationNote: Confirmed by PBS, Al Jazeera, Axios.

text: OIL CRASH: Brent plunged ~16% to ~$93/bbl in after-hours — biggest single-day drop since the 1991 Gulf War. WTI fell ~19% to ~$92. By morning trading both rebounded: Brent ~$109, WTI ~$113. Markets skeptical ceasefire will hold. S&P 500 futures +2.4%.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Axios, NBC News, Yahoo Finance.

text: KHAMENEI HEALTH: US-Israeli intel memo shared with Gulf allies says Mojtaba Khamenei 'unconscious' and 'in severe condition' in Qom. Never appeared publicly since Feb 28. Only AI-generated video released. IRGC effectively managing country's affairs.
verificationNote: Reported by JPost, Breitbart, Yahoo News. Iran denies but provides no proof of life.

text: ISLAMABAD TALKS: Pakistan PM Sharif confirms peace talks begin Friday April 10 in Islamabad. VP Vance expected to lead US delegation. FM Asim Munir was in contact 'all night' with Vance and Witkoff. Goal: conclusive agreement within two-week ceasefire window.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera.

text: NETANYAHU RESISTS: Israeli PM reportedly asked Trump NOT to accept ceasefire, citing 'significant risks.' Israel not formally party to the agreement. IDF operations in Lebanon continue unabated. Ground troops pushing deeper into southern Lebanon.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, JPost.

text: FACT CHECK: Trump claimed Iran has 'no anti-aircraft' weaponry — FALSE. Iran shot down a US F-15E on April 2-3 using new air defense system. Iran claimed it shot down F-35 — also FALSE per CENTCOM: 'All US fighter aircraft are accounted for.' Debris was F-15E.
verificationNote: Confirmed by CNN fact check, Al Jazeera.

text: CEASEFIRE VIOLATED: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel and Gulf states ~10 minutes AFTER Trump's ceasefire announcement. Sirens across Israel. Jubail petrochemical area in Saudi Arabia struck. Habshan gas fire in UAE. Bahrain sirens activated. 4 injured Tel Sheva. IRGC units may not have received stand-down order in time.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, CNBC confirmed missile barrage and Gulf impacts.

text: KEY QUESTION: Did IRGC field commanders receive Khamenei's ceasefire order before launching? Iran's command structure has been fragmented throughout the war with individual commanders making targeting decisions. Whether compliance holds depends on IRGC discipline, not just Khamenei's word.
verificationNote: Multiple analysts raising IRGC chain-of-command concerns.

text: OIL MARKET: Brent stabilized at $95.80 after crashing 16% to ~$93 overnight. Physical dated Brent hit an all-time record of $144.42/barrel -- highest since Platts began publishing in 1987. The gap between futures and physical oil prices is unprecedented, reflecting extreme physical market tightness even as futures fell on ceasefire hopes.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, OilPriceAPI confirmed prices.

text: LEBANON EXCLUDED: Netanyahu formally states ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon.' IDF continuing operations against Hezbollah. Contradicts Pakistan PM Sharif's claim of 'ceasefire everywhere including Lebanon.' This is a major loophole -- Israel can continue its northern front war while the US-Iran ceasefire holds.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post confirmed Netanyahu statement.

text: CEASEFIRE STATUS UPDATE (Day 40 PM): Ceasefire appears to be holding as of April 8 morning. US suspended all offensive strikes. Khamenei ordered all military units to cease fire -- state TV: 'This is not the end of the war.' SNSC: 'Nearly all objectives achieved.' Both sides claiming victory. Islamabad talks Friday April 10.
verificationNote: PBS, Organiser, Axios confirmed ceasefire holding.

text: Iran's 10-point peace plan is maximalist: sanctions lift, frozen assets returned, US withdrawal, Hormuz under Iranian control, right to nuclear enrichment. Notably ABSENT: any commitment to end nuclear program -- the core US/Israeli demand. This ceasefire buys time but the fundamental issues are unresolved. Islamabad talks will be extremely difficult.
verificationNote: The National, Al Jazeera published 10-point plan details.

text: Lavan Island refinery struck hours after ceasefire. Fire visible on satellite imagery. No casualties due to prior evacuation. Attacker unconfirmed but timing suggests this was a pre-planned strike that landed after the ceasefire took effect.
verificationNote: WTOP, LBC confirmed Lavan refinery attack.

text: Iran retaliating against Kuwait, UAE, and Bahrain with drones and missiles AFTER the ceasefire. Kuwait intercepted 28 drones. Bahrain shot down 31 drones and 6 missiles. 2 Bahraini civilians injured. This is exactly the kind of escalation that could collapse the ceasefire before it starts.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Arab Times confirmed Gulf state attacks post-ceasefire.

text: First commercial vessels transiting Hormuz since ceasefire: Greek NJ Earth at 08:44 UTC, Liberian Daytona Beach at 06:59 UTC. Only 10 total movements vs. 135/day peacetime. 800+ vessels still trapped. Iran demanding coordination with armed forces. This is barely a crack in the door.
verificationNote: MarineTraffic, Bloomberg confirmed vessel movements.

text: Trump's 'regime change' declaration is aspirational, not factual. Pezeshkian is still president, IRGC leadership largely intact, Khamenei (or Mojtaba) still issuing orders via state TV. Iran's Farsi ceasefire text claims enrichment rights preserved. Massive gap between Trump and Iranian versions of the deal.
verificationNote: PolitiFact rated Trump's Iran claims misleading. Al Jazeera published ceasefire term comparison.

text: Hegseth's Pentagon briefing claiming 'every single objective achieved' directly contradicts WaPo reporting that more than half of Iran's missile arsenal remains intact. CSIS estimates Iran retained 60-70% of mobile launcher capability. The gap between rhetoric and reality is the most dangerous thing about this ceasefire.
verificationNote: WaPo, The Hill confirmed Hegseth briefing and contradictions.

text: Satellite imagery confirms IDF admits Tehran synagogue Rafi Niya was 'completely destroyed' — collateral damage from strike targeting Khatam al-Anbiya commander. This happened during Passover. Torah scrolls visible under rubble. Iranian Jewish MP condemns Israel. Unprecedented event.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera confirmed IDF admission.
